ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: ID for the Experiment/settings/run
Number of trial within entire task
Is the block a gain block or a loss block? ( Gain block: subjects wins 25 points for winning and 0 for losing, Loss block: subject loses 25 points for losing and 0 points for winning)
Notes: 1 = Gain; -1 = Loss

ElementDescription: Probability of stimulus 1 resulting in a win
Notes: Probability must be decimal value.

ElementDescription: Probability of stimulus 2 resulting in a win
Notes: Probability must be decimal value.

ElementDescription: Probability of stimulus 3 resulting in a win
Notes: Probability must be decimal value.

ElementDescription: Did stimulus 1 result in a win?
Notes: 0 = No; 1 = Yes

ElementDescription: Did stimulus 2 result in a win?
Notes: 0 = No; 1 = Yes

ElementDescription: Did stimulus 3 result in a win?
Notes: 0 = No; 1 = Yes

ElementDescription: Number of points earned if stimulus 1 is selected
Notes: Possible values are 0 and 25 in Gain blocks, and -25 and 0 in Loss blocks. Missed trials are -50 points.

ElementDescription: Number of points earned if stimulus 2 is selected
Notes: Possible values are 0 and 25 in Gain blocks, and -25 and 0 in Loss blocks. Missed trials are -50 points.

ElementDescription: Number of points earned if stimulus 3 is selected
Notes: Possible values are 0 and 25 in Gain blocks, and -25 and 0 in Loss blocks. Missed trials are -50 points.

ElementDescription: Index of left stimulus, sampled from set of 3 stimuli available during current block
Notes: 1 = stimulus 1 selected; 2 = stimulus 2 selected; 3 = stimulus 3 selected

ElementDescription: Index of right stimulus, sampled from set of 3 stimuli available during current block
Notes: 1 = stimulus 1 selected; 2 = stimulus 2 selected; 3 = stimulus 3 selected

ElementDescription: ID of left stimulus in the set of all stimuli shown during the task
ID of right stimulus in the set of all stimuli shown during the task
Gain/Loss Task - Subject's response
Notes: 1 = Left; 2 = Right; -999 = Miss (subject took longer than 2s to respond)

ElementDescription: ID of selected stimulus in the set of all stimuli shown during the task, indexed from 1
Notes: NaN indicates missed response (subject took longer than 2s)

ElementDescription: Index of selected stimulus in the set of 3 stimuli available during current block, indexed from 1
Notes: 1 = stimulus 1 selected; 2 = stimulus 2 selected; 3 = stimulus 3 selected; -999 indicates missed response (subject took longer than 2s)

ElementDescription: Indicates whether selected stimulus had the higher win probability of the two stimuli shown
Notes: 0 = False, 1 = True. -999 indicates missed response (subject took longer than 2s)

ElementDescription: Indicates whether selected stimulus resulted in a win
Notes: 0 = False, 1 = True. -999 indicates missed response (subject took longer than 2s)

ElementDescription: Number of points earned by subject response
Notes: Possible values are 0 and 25 in Gain blocks, and -25 and 0 in Loss blocks. Missed trials are -50 points.

ElementDescription: Time elapsed between stimulus onset and subject reponse
Notes: In milliseconds. -999 indicates missed response (subject took longer than 2s)

ElementDescription: ID for session
Notes: For in-lab subjects, sessionID corresponds to the visit number. For online subjects, sessionID = 1

ElementDescription: Is the trial a practice trial?
Notes: 0 = main task; 1 = practice

ElementDescription: Number of trials where participant receives a loss outcome
Number of trials where participant receives a neutral outcome
Number of trials where participant receives no special outcome
Number of trials where participant response time was faster than 200 ms
Trials where participant may receive reward - average response time
Trials where participant may receive a loss - average response time
Trials where participant may receive a neutral outcome - average response time
Trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a high probability of positive reward - average response time
Trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a low probability of positive reward - average response time
All trials - average response time
Trials where participant may receive reward - average response time after applying Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function to outliers
Notes: The Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function excludes trials where the response time deviated from participant median response time by more than 3.32 times the median absolute deviation

ElementDescription: Trials where participant may receive a loss - average response time after applying Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function to outliers
Notes: The Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function excludes trials where the response time deviated from participant median response time by more than 3.32 times the median absolute deviation

ElementDescription: Trials where participant may receive a neutral outcome - average response time after applying Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function to outliers
Notes: The Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function excludes trials where the response time deviated from participant median response time by more than 3.32 times the median absolute deviation

ElementDescription: Trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a high probability of positive reward - average response time after applying Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function to outliers
Notes: The Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function excludes trials where the response time deviated from participant median response time by more than 3.32 times the median absolute deviation

ElementDescription: Trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a low probability of positive reward - average response time after applying Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function to outliers
Notes: The Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function excludes trials where the response time deviated from participant median response time by more than 3.32 times the median absolute deviation

ElementDescription: All trials - average response time after applying Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function to outliers
Notes: The Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function excludes trials where the response time deviated from participant median response time by more than 3.32 times the median absolute deviation

ElementDescription: Difference between average response time of trials where participant may receive a reward and trials where participant may receive a loss, after applying Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function to outliers
Notes: BAN_wkRTRew-BAN_wkRTLoss

ElementDescription: Difference between average response time of trials where participant may receive a reward and trials where participant may receive a neutral outcome, after applying Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function to outliers
Notes: BAN_wkRTRew-BAN_wkRTNeut

ElementDescription: Difference between average response time of trials where participant may receive a loss and trials where participant may receive a neutral outcome, after applying Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function to outliers
Notes: BAN_wkRTLoss-BAN_wkRTNeut

ElementDescription: Difference between average response time of trials where participant may receive a reward and trials where participant may receive a loss
Notes: BAN_rawRTRew-BAN_rawRTLoss

ElementDescription: Task version
Difference between average response time of trials where participant may receive a reward and trials where participant may receive a neutral outcome
Notes: BAN_rawRTRew-BAN_rawRTNeut

ElementDescription: Difference between average response time of trials where participant may receive a loss and trials where participant may receive a neutral outcome
Notes: BAN_rawRTLoss-BAN_rawRTNeut

ElementDescription: Number of trials participant skipped (did not make a response) during the task
Trials where participants may win reward - Percentage of trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a high probability of getting a reward
Trials where participants may lose reward - Percentage of trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a high probability of avoiding a loss
Trials where participants may see a neutral outcome - Percentage of trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a high probability of the neutral outcome
All trials - Percentage where participant picked the stimulus that has a high probability of positive outcome (reward, avoid loss, or special neutral)
Number of trials where participant receives a rewarding outcome
Out of the trials where participants may win reward, the percentage of trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 60% probability of getting a reward
Out of the trials where participants may have a shock outcome, the percentage of trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 80% probability of not getting a shock (neutral)
Out of the trials where participants may have a shock outcome, the percentage of trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 70% probability of not getting a shock (neutral)
Out of the trials where participants may have a shock outcome, the percentage of trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 60% probability of not getting a shock (neutral)
The total number of trials where participant receive a rewarding outcome in the version where participants are likely to receive a reward 80% of the time they choose the correct stimulus
The total number of trials where participant receive a rewarding outcome in the version where participants are likely to receive a reward 70% of the time they choose the correct stimulus
The total number of trials where participant receive a rewarding outcome in the version where participants are likely to receive a reward 70% of the time they choose the correct stimulus
The total number of trials where participant receive a shock outcome in the version where participants are likely to receive a shock 40% of the time they choose the incorrect stimulus
The total number of trials where participant receive a shock outcome in the version where participants are likely to receive a shock 30% of the time they choose the incorrect stimulus
The total number of trials where participant receive a shock outcome in the version where participants are likely to receive a shock 20% of the time they choose the incorrect stimulus
Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 80% probability of a reward
Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 20% probability of a reward
Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 70% probability of a reward
Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 30% probability of a reward
Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 60% probability of a reward
Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 40% probability of a reward
Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 80% probability of a neutral outcome when the participant picked the correct stimulus in a shock trial
Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 20% probability of a neutral outcome when the participant picked the correct stimulus in a shock trial
Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 70% probability of a neutral outcome when the participant picked the correct stimulus in a shock trial
Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 30% probability of a neutral outcome when the participant picked the correct stimulus in a shock trial
The outcome the participant receives (shock, reward, neutral)
Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 60% probability of a neutral outcome when the participant picked the correct stimulus in a shock trial
Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 40% probability of a neutral outcome when the participant picked the correct stimulus in a shock trial
Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 80% probability of a reward after trimming outlier response time
Notes: The Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function excludes trials where the response time deviated from participant median response time by more than 3.32 times the median absolute deviation

ElementDescription: Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 20% probability of a reward after trimming outlier response time
Notes: The Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function excludes trials where the response time deviated from participant median response time by more than 3.32 times the median absolute deviation

ElementDescription: Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 70% probability of a reward after trimming outlier response time
Notes: The Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function excludes trials where the response time deviated from participant median response time by more than 3.32 times the median absolute deviation

ElementDescription: Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 30% probability of a reward after trimming outlier response time
Notes: The Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function excludes trials where the response time deviated from participant median response time by more than 3.32 times the median absolute deviation

ElementDescription: Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 60% probability of a reward after trimming outlier response time
Notes: The Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function excludes trials where the response time deviated from participant median response time by more than 3.32 times the median absolute deviation

ElementDescription: Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 40% probability of a reward after trimming outlier response time
Notes: The Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function excludes trials where the response time deviated from participant median response time by more than 3.32 times the median absolute deviation

ElementDescription: Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 80% probability of a neutral outcome when the participant picked the correct stimulus in a shock trial after trimming outlier response time
Notes: The Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function excludes trials where the response time deviated from participant median response time by more than 3.32 times the median absolute deviation

ElementDescription: Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 20% probability of a neutral outcome when the participant picked the correct stimulus in a shock trial after trimming outlier response time
Notes: The Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function excludes trials where the response time deviated from participant median response time by more than 3.32 times the median absolute deviation

ElementDescription: The stimulus (picture) the participant is shown

Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 70% probability of a neutral outcome when the participant picked the correct stimulus in a shock trial after trimming outlier response time
Notes: The Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function excludes trials where the response time deviated from participant median response time by more than 3.32 times the median absolute deviation

ElementDescription: Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 30% probability of a neutral outcome when the participant picked the correct stimulus in a shock trial after trimming outlier response time
Notes: The Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function excludes trials where the response time deviated from participant median response time by more than 3.32 times the median absolute deviation

ElementDescription: Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 60% probability of a neutral outcome when the participant picked the correct stimulus in a shock trial after trimming outlier response time
Notes: The Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function excludes trials where the response time deviated from participant median response time by more than 3.32 times the median absolute deviation

ElementDescription: Average response time across trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 40% probability of a neutral outcome when the participant picked the correct stimulus in a shock trial after trimming outlier response time
Notes: The Wilcox-Kesselman trimming function excludes trials where the response time deviated from participant median response time by more than 3.32 times the median absolute deviation

ElementDescription: Total # Trials
The number of reward trials participant skipped (did not make a response) during the task
The number of shock trials participant skipped (did not make a response) during the task
Out of the trials where participants may win reward, the percentage of trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 80% probability of getting a reward
Out of the trials where participants may win reward, the percentage of trials where participant picked the stimulus that has a 70% probability of getting a reward
